name: Spinach Gomaae (Japanese Sesame Spinach)
description: Blanched spinach dressed in a nutty toasted-sesame sauce — a classic Japanese vegetable side.
recipeInstructions: Blanch the spinach 30 seconds, shock in ice water, then squeeze out all excess water and cut into 1-inch lengths.
Grind most of the toasted sesame seeds in a mortar; mix with soy sauce and sugar to make the dressing.
Toss the spinach in the sesame dressing and top with the remaining whole seeds.
author: Just One Cookbook

ai.cliffNotes: Fresh-toasted sesame, stem-first blanching, and an ice shock give bright, properly-seasoned gomaae.
keyTips: Toast the sesame seeds fresh (even if pre-toasted) for nutty depth.
Blanch stems first (15-20s), then leaves (30-45s), so nothing overcooks.
Shock in ice water right after to set the color and remove the oxalic bite.
